riddles.riddle: I am a wrought-iron lattice tower in Paris, originally built for the 1889 World's Fair. What is my name?
riddles.description: Magnifique! You've found the 'Iron Lady.' Fun fact: she was originally intended to be a temporary structure, but her height made her perfect for radio transmissions!

riddles.riddle: I am a massive ancient wall that winds across thousands of miles to protect the northern borders of China. What am I called?
riddles.description: Impressive! You've conquered the longest structure ever built by humans. It is actually a collection of many walls built over several centuries.

riddles.riddle: I am a colossal copper statue standing in New York Harbor, holding a torch high as a symbol of freedom. Who am I?
riddles.description: Excellent! A gift from France to the U.S., she has welcomed millions of people to American shores since 1886. Her official name is 'Liberty Enlightening the World.'

riddles.riddle: Located in Giza, Egypt, I am the oldest and largest of the three pyramids and was the tallest man-made structure for over 3,800 years. What am I?
riddles.description: A monumental achievement! You've solved the mystery of the last remaining wonder of the ancient world, built for the Pharaoh Khufu.
